1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Simon Peter, a servant and an apostle of Jesus Christ. To those who have received a faith as precious as ours, through the goodness of our God and Saviour Jesus Christ:
May God give you more and more grace and peace, as you come to know God and Jesus our Lord.
God's own power has given us everything we need to live a life that pleases him. He did this by helping us know the One who called us by his own glory and goodness.
Through that glory and goodness, God gave us his great and precious promises. By these promises you can share in God's own nature and escape the ruin that evil desires bring into the world.
So do your best to add to your faith. To your faith, add goodness; to goodness, add knowledge;
to knowledge, add self-control; to self-control, add patience; to patience, add a godly life;
to a godly life, add kindness to your brothers and sisters; and to that kindness, add love.
If you have these things, and they keep growing, they will keep you from being useless. They will help you truly know our Lord Jesus Christ.
But anyone who does not have these things is blind. He cannot see clearly, and he has forgotten that his old sins were washed away.
So, brothers and sisters, work hard to make your calling and your place with God sure. If you do these things, you will never fall.
Then you will be given a rich welcome into the kingdom that lasts forever, the kingdom of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ.
So I will always remind you of these things, even though you already know them and stand firm in the truth you have.
I think it is right to keep reminding you as long as I live in this body.
I know that soon I must leave this body, as our Lord Jesus Christ has shown me.
So I will do my best to make sure that, after I am gone, you will always remember these things.
When we told you about the power of our Lord Jesus Christ and his coming, we were not following clever made-up stories. We saw his greatness with our own eyes.
God the Father gave Jesus honor and glory. A voice came to Jesus from the great glory of God, saying: This is my Son, whom I love. I am very pleased with him.
We ourselves heard that voice from heaven, because we were with Jesus on the holy mountain.

So we are even more sure of the message the prophets spoke. You will do well to pay attention to it. It is like a light shining in a dark place, until the day dawns and the morning star rises in your hearts.
Above all, you must understand that no prophecy of Scripture came from a prophet's own idea.
For prophecy never came simply because a person wanted it to. Instead, people spoke from God as the Holy Spirit led them.

2.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: But there were also false prophets among the people, just as there will be false teachers among you. They will quietly bring in destructive lies. They will even say no to the Master who bought them, and they will bring quick ruin on themselves.
Many people will follow their shameful ways, and because of them, others will speak evil of the way of truth.
These teachers are greedy. They will use clever lies to make money from you. But their judgment has been ready for a long time, and their ruin is not asleep.
God did not spare the angels when they sinned. He threw them down to hell and kept them in chains of darkness, to wait for judgment.
And God did not spare the ancient world. But he saved Noah, who preached what is right, along with seven others, when he brought the flood on the world of sinful people.
God turned the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah into ashes. He condemned them, and made them an example of what will happen to sinful people.
But God rescued Lot, a good man who was troubled by the filthy lives of the evil people around him.
(Day after day, this good man lived among them and was deeply troubled in his soul by the evil things he saw and heard.)
So the Lord knows how to rescue godly people from their troubles, and how to hold sinful people for punishment on the day of judgment.
This is especially true for those who follow their dirty desires and hate authority. These teachers are bold and proud. They are not afraid to insult powerful spirits.
Yet even angels, who are far stronger and more powerful, do not bring insulting charges against those spirits before the Lord.
But these false teachers insult what they do not understand. They are like wild animals that act without thinking, born only to be caught and destroyed. And like animals, they too will be destroyed.
They will be paid back with harm for the harm they have done. They love to enjoy themselves in broad daylight. They are stains and spots among you, enjoying their lies while they eat with you.
Their eyes are full of adultery; they never stop sinning. They trap people who are weak. Their hearts are trained to be greedy. They are under God's curse.
They have left the right way and wandered off. They have followed the way of Balaam son of Beor, who loved to be paid for doing wrong.
But a donkey corrected him for his sin. The donkey, which cannot talk, spoke with a human voice and stopped the prophet's madness.
These false teachers are like wells with no water, like clouds blown away by a storm. The deepest darkness has been kept for them.
They brag with empty, proud words. By appealing to sinful desires, they trap people who are just escaping from those who live wrongly.
They promise these people freedom, while they themselves are slaves to what is rotten. For a person is a slave to whatever controls him.

These people had escaped the filth of the world by knowing our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ. But if they get caught in it again and are beaten by it, they are worse off at the end than they were at the start.
It would have been better for them never to have known the right way, than to know it and then turn away from the holy command that was given to them.
What the true sayings say has happened to them: A dog returns to its own vomit, and: A washed pig goes back to rolling in the mud.

3.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Dear friends, this is now my second letter to you. In both letters I have tried to wake up your honest minds by reminding you of things.
I want you to remember the words spoken long ago by the holy prophets, and the command of our Lord and Saviour that came through your apostles.
First of all, you must understand that in the last days people will come who make fun of the truth and follow their own evil desires.
They will say: Jesus promised to come back. So where is he? Ever since our ancestors died, everything has stayed the same, just as it has been since the world was made.
But they choose to forget that long ago God made the heavens by his word, and made the earth out of water and through water.
And by that same water, the world of that time was flooded and destroyed.
By that same word of God, the heavens and earth that exist now are being kept for fire. They are being kept for the day of judgment, when sinful people will be destroyed.
But do not forget this one thing, dear friends: with the Lord, one day is like a thousand years, and a thousand years are like one day.
The Lord is not slow to keep his promise, as some people think of slowness. Instead, he is patient with you. He does not want anyone to be lost, but wants everyone to turn away from sin.

But the day of the Lord will come like a thief. On that day the heavens will disappear with a roar; everything will be burned up and melt in the great heat, and the earth and all that is in it will be laid bare.
Since everything will be destroyed in this way, think what kind of people you should be. You should live holy and godly lives,
as you wait for the day of God and look forward to it. On that day the heavens will be set on fire and destroyed, and everything will melt in the heat.
But we are waiting for what God promised: a new heaven and a new earth, where what is right will live.
So then, dear friends, while you wait for this, do your best to be found by God at peace, without sin, and with nothing against you.
Remember that our Lord is patient so that people can be saved. Our dear brother Paul wrote to you about this too, with the wisdom God gave him.

He writes the same way in all his letters when he speaks about these things. Some of the things in his letters are hard to understand. People who do not know better, and who are not firm in faith, twist his words — and they twist the other Scriptures too — and so they destroy themselves.
So then, dear friends, since you already know this, be careful. Do not let the mistakes of evil people lead you away and cause you to fall from your firm faith.
Instead, keep growing in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ. To him be glory both now and forever. Amen.
